chapter_question: How does ambient projection create the first model of parallel transport?
inspection_targets: Ambient motion is repeatedly projected back into tangent planes.
Normals and tangent planes show where projection happens.
A geodesic keeps its tangent self-parallel.
The physical strip analogy becomes a measurable development.

source_span.focus: Builds parallel transport extrinsically by projecting motion into the surface and by physical transport analogies.
source_span.label: Chapter 22
source_span.title: Extrinsic Constructions
topics: Projecting vectors back into the tangent surface
Transport along curves embedded in space
Geodesics as curves with self-parallel tangents
Extrinsic intuition for a later intrinsic definition
